frameworks.name: higher-order theory of consciousness
frameworks.one_line: consciousness arises from higher-order representations tied to subjective belief

frameworks.name: perceptual reality monitoring
frameworks.one_line: mechanism distinguishing percepts from internally generated representations

frameworks.name: global availability theories
frameworks.one_line: consciousness as global broadcasting of information across the brain

frameworks.name: biological / representational primitivism
frameworks.one_line: treats conscious phenomenology as a brute biological or representational fact

core_question: What characterizes conscious perception if not global availability or brute biological/representational properties?
key_assumptions: consciousness must connect to belief formation and epistemic justification
metacognition is integral to characterizing conscious perception
higher-order accounts can be made empirically grounded
interpretations: characterizing consciousness requires linking it to subjective belief and epistemic justification
an empirically-grounded higher-order theory can supplant global-availability and primitivist accounts
